Appendices A–E are integral to this policy. All five are referenced in specific sections and must be completed or maintained as directed. Blank templates are maintained in the Policy Asset Library. Completed versions are retained as ISO/IEC 42001 and regulatory evidence.

Appendix A
Approved AI Tools Register
The only AI tools permitted for organizational use are those listed in this register with a current Active status. This register is maintained as a live document by the AI Governance Lead. Any tool not in this register is prohibited under Section 8.

How to use this register: Before using any AI tool for organizational work, check this register. Confirm the tool is listed with Active status. Confirm the data classification permitted for that tool covers the data you intend to use. Confirm the use tier permitted. If your use case requires a higher tier than listed, submit a Tier 3 or Tier 4 authorization request per Section 7. If the tool is not listed, follow the process to submit it for consideration before use — not after.

To request tool addition: Submit to the AI Governance Lead with: tool name, provider, intended use case, data classification required, estimated users, link to provider privacy and data processing documentation. Assessment completed within 10 business days.

Tool Name Provider Category Risk Category Approved For (Functions) Max Data Classification DPA in Place Approval Date Review Date
[Tool Name] _______ Generative AI / LLM Cat 4 All functions — writing assistance, summarization Internal Yes _______ _______
[Tool Name] _______ Code AI Cat 4 Engineering · Product Internal Yes _______ _______
[Tool Name] _______ Embedded AI (SaaS) Cat 3 All functions — productivity suite AI features Confidential Yes _______ _______
[Tool Name] _______ AI Transcription Cat 3 All functions — meetings and calls (with participant notification) Internal Yes _______ _______
Additional rows added as tools are approved. Contact the AI Governance Lead to request tool assessment. No tool is approved for use until it appears in this register with Active status.
Status codes: Active = currently approved for listed uses and data classifications. Under Review = assessment in progress, not yet approved for use. Suspended = temporarily removed pending risk review or provider assessment. Decommissioned = no longer approved, migrate to alternative tool listed in register notes.
Last updated by: _________________________ · Date: _______________
AI Governance Lead sign-off: _________________________ · Date: _______________
Appendix B
Prohibited Use Examples
The seven prohibitions in Section 8 are stated as principles. This appendix gives operational examples by function — to answer the question "does this apply to what I do?" before the behavior occurs, not after.
ProhibitionFunction-Specific ScenarioWhy This Is ProhibitedWhat to Do Instead
#1 Social Scoring Using an AI tool to generate a "trustworthiness score" or "engagement prediction" for employees or customers based on their past behavior, communication patterns, or social connections. This is social scoring — the use of AI to evaluate individuals based on behavioral patterns in ways that are opaque, unchallengeable, and potentially discriminatory. Prohibited regardless of the intent behind the scoring. Use defined performance metrics and documented criteria. Any performance assessment system must be transparent, challengeable, and administered by a human with accountability for the decision.
#2 Manipulation Using an AI tool to design marketing messages that exploit identified psychological vulnerabilities in target audience segments — for example, targeting users identified as anxious or impulsive with urgency-based messaging calibrated to their specific vulnerability. This is the deliberate use of AI to manipulate rather than inform. The identification of psychological vulnerabilities through AI and the calibration of messaging to exploit them is prohibited, regardless of the commercial objective. Design marketing that informs, persuades, and provides genuine value. Targeting based on behavior and preference is permitted; targeting calibrated to exploit psychological vulnerabilities is not.
#3 Unauthorized Data A finance team member pastes a client's financial statements into a general-purpose AI tool to generate analysis — without checking whether that tool is authorized for Confidential data in Appendix A. Client financial statements are Confidential data. Entering them into a tool not authorized for that classification is a data governance breach. The fact that the output was useful does not change the classification of the breach. Check Appendix A before entering any client data into an AI tool. If no approved tool covers the use case, submit a Tier 3 authorization request to the AI Governance Lead before proceeding.
#4 Deceptive Content Using an AI voice cloning tool to produce a voicemail from a sales director to prospective clients, without disclosing that the voice was AI-generated and not a live recording from the named individual. This is the use of AI to deceive recipients about the origin and nature of the communication. The deception is material — the recipient would reasonably treat a message from a named individual differently from a synthesized message. The reputational and legal exposure is severe. If AI-generated voice content is used in communications, it must be disclosed. If the content is generated by a named individual's cloned voice, that individual's explicit consent and the recipient's awareness of the AI generation are both required.
#5 Discrimination Using an AI recruitment screening tool that, without the organization's awareness, scores candidates from certain educational institutions lower because those institutions are proxies for demographic characteristics in the training data. The discrimination is not less illegal because it operates through a proxy rather than a direct characteristic. The organization's obligation is to ensure that AI-assisted recruitment decisions do not discriminate — including through proxies. This requires active testing and monitoring, not assumption of compliance. Audit AI recruitment tools for demographic bias before deployment. Human review of all AI-assisted screening decisions. Appendix A assessment for recruitment tools includes bias testing as a condition of approval.
#6 Security Probing An engineer uses an AI coding tool to generate a script that probes the organization's own API for vulnerabilities, without coordinating with the security team or obtaining authorization under the penetration testing policy. AI-assisted security probing without authorization is indistinguishable from malicious activity from a detection and response perspective. It also creates liability — a tool designed to identify vulnerabilities that is used without authorization may itself exploit vulnerabilities in ways the user does not intend. All security testing — including AI-assisted testing — must be conducted under the penetration testing policy and with IT security authorization. Contact the security team before using any AI tool for vulnerability identification or security testing.
#7 Shadow AI A consultant uses a personal subscription to an AI research tool — one not in Appendix A — to analyze market data for a client engagement, because it produces better summaries than the approved tool and they are under time pressure. The tool is not approved. Its data handling has not been assessed. There is no DPA. The organization cannot audit the data that was shared. The output quality is irrelevant to the governance failure. Time pressure does not create a governance exception. Use the approved tool, even if its output requires more editing. If the approved tool is genuinely inadequate for the use case, submit a tool assessment request. If the situation is truly urgent, submit an exception request under Section 15 — which takes 10 business days, so plan ahead.
Working Instruments
The Governance Forms
Appendix C
Risk Classification Matrix
The full risk classification instrument. Use this matrix when assessing any AI tool or use case for Tier 3 or Tier 4 authorization. Complete one matrix per tool or use case assessed. File with the authorization record.
Assessment FieldEntry
Tool or use case being assessed_______________________
AI model or provider_______________________
Business function requesting_______________________
Requesting individual_______________________
Assessment date_______________________
AI Governance Lead conducting assessment_______________________
Step 1Preliminary Classification Check
QuestionAnswer (Yes/No)Implication If Yes
Does this system make or directly inform decisions affecting individuals' fundamental rights, employment, credit, health, or essential services access?_______→ Category 1 mandatory. Stop here.
Is this an agentic AI system — can it take autonomous actions without per-action human approval?_______→ Category 2 minimum mandatory (Agentic AI Override, Section 11.3). Apply Category 2 controls.
Does this system process personal data at scale, or operate as a customer-facing AI agent?_______→ Category 2 minimum. Apply Category 2 controls.
Has the system been classified as High-Risk under the EU AI Act (Annex III)?_______→ Category 1. Regulatory conformity assessment required.
Step 2Likelihood × Impact Scoring
Risk FactorLikelihood (1–4)Impact (1–4)Risk Score (L×I)
Data breach or unauthorized data exposure through the AI tool_______ / 4_______ / 4_______
AI-generated output causing harm to an individual_______ / 4_______ / 4_______
Discriminatory output affecting a protected group_______ / 4_______ / 4_______
Regulatory non-compliance (EU AI Act, GDPR, DPDP Act)_______ / 4_______ / 4_______
Reputational harm from AI-generated output entering public domain_______ / 4_______ / 4_______
IP ownership dispute arising from AI-generated outputs_______ / 4_______ / 4_______
Maximum risk score (highest individual score)Take the highest individual score — do not average_______
Score thresholds: 1–3 = Low (Category 4 controls). 4–5 = Moderate (Category 3 minimum). 6–8 = High (Category 2 minimum). 9–16 = Critical (Category 1 minimum, CCO review mandatory).
Step 3Final Classification and Required Controls
FieldEntry
Preliminary classification from Step 1 (if triggered)_______________________
Risk score from Step 2 (maximum score)_______________________
Final classification (higher of Step 1 and Step 2)Cat 1  Cat 2  Cat 3  Cat 4   (circle one)
Tier authorization requiredTier 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 (per Section 7)
DPA required?Yes / No
Legal review required?Yes / No — reason: _______________________
External regulatory conformity assessment required?Yes / No — standard: _______________________
Human oversight mechanism defined_______________________
Review frequency for this tool/use case_______________________
Assessed by (AI Governance Lead): _________________________    Date: _______________
Approved by (CCO): _________________________    Date: _______________
Appendix D
AI Incident Report Form
Complete this form for all AI incidents and near-misses. Submit to the AI Governance Lead within 24 hours of identification. Reporter protection applies unconditionally to good-faith reports.
Part 1 — Incident Identification
Incident ReferenceAI-INC-___-___-______     Date Reported: _______________
Reported ByName: _________________     Role: _________________     Department: _________________
Date and Time of Incident_______________
AI Tool InvolvedTool name: _________________     Is it in Appendix A? Yes / No (Shadow AI if No)
Incident Type☐ Data breach / exposure    ☐ Discriminatory output    ☐ Hallucination acted upon    ☐ Shadow AI use    ☐ Agentic AI out-of-scope action    ☐ Prohibited use (Section 8 #___)    ☐ Near-miss    ☐ Other: _______________
Severity Classification☐ Critical    ☐ Major    ☐ Minor    ☐ Near-Miss      (AI Governance Lead confirms on receipt)
Part 2 — Incident Description
What happened?Describe the incident or near-miss in factual terms: what the AI tool was asked to do, what it produced or did, and what happened as a result.

 
 
Who or what was affected?Individuals (describe without names): _______________________   Organization: _______________________   Client: _______________________
Was the AI output acted upon?Yes / No — If yes: _______________________ (describe what action was taken based on the output)
For near-misses: how was the harm prevented?_______________________ (describe the safeguard, catch mechanism, or human review that prevented the harm)
Data involvedClassification level: Public / Internal / Confidential / Restricted     Volume: _______________________
Part 3 — Root Cause and Containment
Immediate containment action taken(What was done within the first hour to limit harm?)
 
Root cause — Why 1 
Root cause — Why 2 
Root cause — Why 3 
Root cause — Why 4 
Root cause — Why 5 / Root Cause IdentifiedROOT CAUSE:
 
 
Part 4 — Response and Notification
Corrective action planActions to eliminate the root cause: _______________________
Owner: _______________________   Target date: _______________________
Preventive actionActions to prevent recurrence across other tools / functions: _______________________
EU AI Act GPAI notification assessmentDoes this incident involve a GPAI model? Yes / No     If yes, does it require provider notification under Article 73? Yes / No / Assess with Legal
Provider notified: Yes / No / NA     Date: _______________________
Regulatory reporting assessmentDoes this incident require notification to a regulatory body (GDPR supervisory authority, EU AI Office, DPDP authority)? Legal assessment required: Yes / No
If yes: body notified: _______________________   Date: _______________________
Internal escalation completedAI Governance Lead notified: Yes — Date: _______    CCO notified: Yes / NA — Date: _______    CEO notified: Yes / NA — Date: _______

AI Governance Lead reviewed: _________________________   Date: _______
Closed by: _________________________   Date: _______
Appendix E
Exception Request Form
Complete and submit to the AI Governance Lead. All fields are required. Incomplete submissions will be returned. Processing time: 10 business days. See Section 15 for what can and cannot be excepted.
Part 1 — Request Identification
Exception ReferenceEXC-___-___-______     Date Submitted: _______________
Requested ByName: _________________    Role: _________________    Department: _________________
Department Head ApprovalName: _________________    Signature: _________________    Date: _________________
Exception Type Requested☐ Use of unapproved AI tool    ☐ Higher data classification than Appendix A permits    ☐ Deviation from Tier requirement    ☐ Other: _______________
Tool or use case affected_______________________
Requested exception periodFrom: _______________    To (sunset date): _______________    (Maximum 90 days — Section 15.3)
Part 2 — Business Justification
Business need — why this exception is required(State the specific business need that cannot be met through approved tools or existing Tier processes.)
 
 
Why an approved tool cannot substitute(Name the closest approved alternative and explain specifically why it is inadequate for this use case.)
 
Duration — why this period is required(Explain why the exception cannot be resolved through the standard approval process within the processing period.)
 
What happens if the exception is not granted?(State the operational consequence of the exception being declined.)
 
Part 3 — Risk Acknowledgement and Controls
Risk classification of the excepted useCategory 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 (assessed using Appendix C methodology)
Data classification involvedPublic / Internal / Confidential / Restricted
DPA status (if personal data involved)In place / Pending / Not required — reason: _______________________
Controls applied during the exception period(List the specific controls that mitigate the risk of the excepted use — human oversight, access restrictions, logging, monitoring.)
 
Risk acknowledgement (Requestor)I confirm that I have read Section 15, that this request does not seek to except any absolute prohibition under Section 8, and that I accept responsibility for ensuring the excepted use operates within the controls stated above.
Signature: _________________________   Date: _______________________

Amendment History
VersionDateAuthorApproved BySummary of Changes
1.0 May 2026 Chief Compliance Officer CEO · Board AI Governance Committee Initial release. Complete 18-section AI Usage Governance Policy for a cross-sector enterprise deploying AI across all business functions. Includes five appendices: Approved AI Tools Register (A), Prohibited Use Examples (B), Risk Classification Matrix (C), AI Incident Report Form (D), and Exception Request Form (E). Aligned with EU AI Act (2024), NIST AI RMF (2023), ISO/IEC 42001 (2023), and India DPDP Act 2023. One policy from a 27-policy AI governance framework.
Future amendments recorded here in chronological order. Minor amendments (0.1 increments): changes to Appendix A, factual corrections, non-substantive clarifications. Material amendments (1.0 increments): changes to Sections 5–13, new or removed sections, changes to covered obligations. All amendments require CCO approval and Board AI Committee notification before publication.
